{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels1 = BASE_CHANNELS
        channels2 = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM

        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels1, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels1))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        downsample_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels1, channels2, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            downsample_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels2))
        downsample_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))

        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            ResidualBlock(channels1, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            *downsample_layers,
            ResidualBlock(channels2, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels2, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Upgrade the single-stage network into a two-stage micro-ResNet with one downsampling transition and a wider second residual stage, improving representation quality while keeping the model compact.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Add modest label smoothing to improve generalization for the now-stronger small ResNet under the fixed 256-step budget, with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Remove the label-smoothing change that regressed validation loss and return to the stronger simpler training objective before testing further ideas.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Switch from Adam to AdamW so weight decay is decoupled from the adaptive updates, which often improves short-horizon generalization on small CNNs without adding code complexity.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Return to the better Adam setting and modestly widen the two-stage micro-ResNet plus MLP head, which should increase representational capacity without a large jump in complexity or compute.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The widened model is training well under warmup plus cosine decay, so a modestly higher peak learning rate may extract more progress within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate improved validation loss, so pushing the peak rate one step further is the most direct follow-up for this short warmup-plus-cosine schedule.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        warmup_steps = max(4, total_steps // 32)","old":"        warmup_steps = max(8, total_steps // 16)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The higher peak learning rate is helping, so shortening warmup should let the model spend more of the fixed 256-step budget near its more effective learning-rate range.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After shortening warmup, the schedule improved again, so the next strongest follow-up is to raise the peak learning rate slightly and test whether the model can use the remaining budget even more aggressively.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Each recent increase in peak learning rate has improved validation loss, so the strongest next move is another modest upward step while keeping the now-effective short warmup schedule unchanged.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Revert the overly aggressive peak learning rate and test slightly weaker weight decay, which may suit the short-budget Adam regime better now that the model has been widened.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The lower weight-decay setting nearly matched the best result, so the next clean follow-up is to reduce it a bit further and test whether this short-budget Adam run benefits from even weaker regularization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Restore the slightly better weight decay and test a larger batch so the model can cover more of CIFAR-10 within the fixed 256-step budget while keeping the rest of the strong configuration unchanged.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"A larger batch materially improved validation loss by exposing the model to more CIFAR-10 examples within the fixed 256-step budget, so the strongest follow-up is to push that tradeoff one step further.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The move to a larger batch improved loss again, so the most promising follow-up is to raise the learning rate to better match the reduced gradient noise at batch size 128.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The recent gains came from increasing batch size under a fixed-step budget, so the highest-value remaining test is another moderate batch increase to cover more training examples while keeping the rest of the improved optimization setup intact.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size increases have produced the largest recent gains under the fixed 256-step evaluator, so the highest-upside final test is one more moderate increase to expose the model to even more training examples.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
